title: Executive Order 10621—Delegation of Certain Functions of the President to the Secretary of Defense

flags.justification: Each of the roughly fourteen delegated authorities is narrow and specific (e.g., approving voluntary enlistment extensions, setting warrant officer numbers), already defined by its own statute — a lateral relocation of existing narrow authorities, not a new broad grant.
Each delegated authority is tied to a specific, precisely-cited statute with defined scope.
Many distinct authorities are delegated together, but all coherently relate to Navy and Marine Corps personnel administration.
A pure delegation mechanism; any discretion involved is inherent to the underlying statutes, not created by this order.
No compliance regime is created by this delegation order.
A standing delegation of routine, permanent personnel-administration authorities, evergreen by nature.
Sec. 3 explicitly preserves all prior presidential actions on these matters until amended under the new delegation.
No third-party compliance regime is created.
Routine administrative delegation of narrow, pre-existing authorities; no novel authority claim.
A delegation from the President to the Secretary of Defense; no other agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. Housekeeping delegation of roughly fourteen narrow, pre-existing statutory personnel authorities.
